title: slot-recon online-check: release slot when GitHub-online but no local container
description: release_stale_slots only frees slots whose runner_id is gone from GitHub; must also handle runner online at GitHub with no local container carrying ezgha.runner_id label. 409 self-heal (c63cc81) masks most cases.

title: re-run 30-min health observation post-409-fix
description: docs/observe-20260704 recorded UNHEALTHY every minute pre-fix. Re-run doctor loop 30min, commit evidence, after fleet converges to 16/16.

title: SECURITY: pull_request trigger added to ci-selfhosted.yml — review/remove
description: Contradicts the file's fork-PR safety comment on a public repo with self-hosted runners. Uncommitted local change as of handoff.

title: rebase+merge 6 tested fix branches (tj2,vmz,xh4,5rz,1fu,ci-selfhosted)
description: All ER'd earlier; predate stable-naming/slot-recon so expect docker_backend.rs conflicts. ci-selfhosted/fix first (main self-hosted CI is red).

title: Non-numeric slot key panics and crashes the serve loop

title: Slot file write is non-atomic — a crash mid-write corrupts the file and wedges all future allocation
description: src/docker_backend.rs:77 — write_slot_assignments uses `std::fs::write(&path, raw)`, which truncates the existing file in place and then writes. If the process is killed (or the daemon/host dies) between truncate and full write...; fix: Write atomically: serialize to a sibling temp file in the same directory (e.g. `slot_assignments.toml.tmp.<pid>`), fsync, then `std::fs::rename(tmp, path)`. rename(2) within a directory is atomic on P...

title: No cross-process locking on the slot file — concurrent next_slot calls double-allocate the same slot
description: src/docker_backend.rs:91 — next_slot does read_slot_assignments (line 91) -> find first vacant (line 92-94) -> insert empty marker -> write (line 96) with no file lock held across the read/write. title: launchctl load -w fails on reinstall (agent already loaded) — install is not idempotent
